hypothesis: If CHROMA is trained with a frozen Llama encoder and a trainable Transformer decoder on templated natural-language descriptions paired with simulated multilayer stacks, then it will translate natural-language prompts into multilayer thin-film designs that reproduce target structural colors with low CIE76 ΔE across paraphrases and novel optical targets, with color-matching performance improving as encoder size and decoder depth increase and remaining robust across viewing angles and layer counts.
